title: campanula tridentata var. ardonensis
intro: Campanula tridentata var. ardonensis, also known as Ardon bellflower, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the Ardon region in Greece. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ardon bellflower include relief from respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and reduce fever. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
